name: Governance and security trade-off
summary: MCP wins multi-tenant deployments: OAuth 2.1, per-user tokens, scoped tools, centralized audit. Raw CLI inherits ambient GH_TOKEN and shell history. Scalekit and enterprise posts recommend CLI for solo dev inner-loop and MCP (or governed gateways) for org-wide agents. CLI Agent Spec and agent-native CLI designs add structured errors and contracts without MCP overhead. GAX targets both: caps + sidecar auth + envelope without schema bloat.
methodology_notes: Synthesis from Scalekit decision framework, Stacklok/Firecrawl enterprise framing, MCP specification auth extensions. Not a single quantitative benchmark.
implications_for_gax: GAX is positioned for outer-loop: gax auth login device flow, cap-from-oauth, macaroons, OTEL audit export. Eval scores governance 0.95 for GAX vs 0.2 CLI vs 0.7 simulated MCP.
